name: Mercadillo, Gueñes and Sodupe
name_original: Mercadillo, Güeñes y Sodupe — circular desde Bilbao
description: This difficult 48.5-mile road cycling route from Bilbao offers scenic views, challenging climbs, and a blend of urban and rural landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Bilbao offer a diverse landscape that blends urban areas with natural beauty. The region features a mix of undulating terrain, challenging mountain passes, scenic coastal roads, and serene river paths. This varied topography provides options for cyclists of all abilities, from demanding climbs to more relaxed rides along waterways. Best road cycling routes around Bilbao The most popular road cycling route is Mercadillo, Gueñes and Sodupe, a 48.5 miles (78.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through varied landscapes, offering a challenging experience. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Views of the Bilbao estuary – View of the Vizcaya Bridge loop from Santutxu, a difficult 56.6 miles (91.1 km) path. This route provides extensive views of the Bilbao estuary and passes the Vizcaya Bridge. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de Humaran – Bicycle Highway Near Bilbao loop from Santutxu, a 46.3 miles (74.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Bilbao is defined by coastal roads, river paths, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to longer, more difficult routes. The routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 9900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bilbao's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bilbao?
A: There are over 65 road cycling routes around Bilbao, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include routes for all skill levels, from easy rides to challenging mountain passes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bilbao?
A: Yes, Bilbao offers several routes suitable for beginners. You can find 9 easy routes, often along river paths or greenways, providing a more relaxed cycling experience. The Nervión River path, for instance, offers an easy cruise through scenic areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Bilbao?
A: Bilbao's road cycling terrain is incredibly diverse, often described as 'Bilbao flat' due to its continuous mix of climbs, descents, and undulating roads. You'll encounter challenging mountain passes, picturesque coastal roads along the Bay of Biscay, and serene river paths.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes near Bilbao?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Bilbao offers 32 difficult routes. These often include significant climbs, such as those leading to Pico Mendibil, Alto de Humaran, or the Orduña mountain pass. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032162641/mercadillo-guenes-and-sodupe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercadillo, Gueñes and Sodupe&lt;/a&gt; provide a demanding experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any scenic coastal road cycling routes from Bilbao?
A: Yes, the Basque coastline near Bilbao is stunning for road cycling. Routes extend to towns like Getxo, Sopela, and Pletzia, offering breathtaking views of the Bay of Biscay. A notable ride is to San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, known for its iconic viewpoint. You can also enjoy routes with extensive views of the Bilbao estuary, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31362109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Bilbao estuary – View of the Vizcaya Bridge loop from Santutxu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while road cycling in Bilbao?
A: Many routes integrate with Bilbao's cultural and natural attractions. You can cycle past the iconic Guggenheim Museum, explore the historic Casco Viejo, or admire the UNESCO World Heritage Vizcaya Bridge. Other points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oxirando Tower&lt;/a&gt;, and various summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganekogorta Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Bilbao?
A: For families or those preferring a more relaxed pace, the riverside paths and greenways are ideal. The Bidegorri de la Ría route along the Nervion River offers a peaceful ride, and the 'Bilbao's Green Belt (GR 228)' provides an escape from the city bustle. These routes are generally flatter and safer for all ages.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Bilbao?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Bilbao are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the Getxo Coastal Loop or routes that explore specific areas like the Montes de Hierro Greenway. You can find several circular options, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38143657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Humaran – Bicycle Highway Near Bilbao loop from Santutxu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How can I access road cycling routes around Bilbao using public transport?
A: Bilbao has a bike-friendly infrastructure, and many routes are accessible from urban centers. While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, the city's commitment to cycling includes bike lanes and paths that connect to various starting points. For detailed information on transporting bikes on public transport, it's best to consult local transport authorities.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bilbao?
A: The diverse climate of the Basque Country means cycling is possible year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for road cycling in Bilbao, with milder temperatures and less rain. Summer can be warm, especially inland, while winter offers cooler rides, though some mountain passes might be more challenging due to weather conditions.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Bilbao?
A: Many road cycling routes around Bilbao start from urban areas or towns with available parking facilities. For routes beginning outside the city center, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or in nearby villages. Bilbao also has public bike-sharing docking stations, which can be useful for shorter urban explorations.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Bilbao?
A: The road cycling routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic coastal views, and the well-maintained paths that make exploring the area a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any routes that combine natural beauty with historical sites?
A: Yes, the region around Bilbao is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. Routes through natural parks like Armañón or Urkiola offer lush forests and rugged mountains, while also passing by historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4018034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hirumugarrieta Megalithic Site&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4212898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de la Quadra&lt;/a&gt;. The Enkarterri - Meatzaldea route, for example, combines green valleys with ancient mining sites, showcasing the area's industrial heritage.
tips: Section of bike lane to pedal pleasantly along the banks of the Bilbao estuary. From the saddle you can see the coexistence of traditional buildings in contrast to industrial and modernist buildings.
Great switch back road leading up Larreineta Funicular stop & beyond. It does never get too steep & good surface all the way, perfect !
Climb around 7% on a quiet road with good asphalt

The most emblematic building of Las Encartaciones. It consists of eight rooms that show the history of the region from prehistory to the 19th century. The visit begins with some images and reproductions of prehistoric remains in the area. Then it goes to an ethnographic part, with objects, clothing, images and models of houses, fortifications and even model reproductions of the towns in the area. It shows an interesting collection of objects and art from the area. It also has an area for temporary exhibitions. Admission is free.

Nice views of the largest town in the province. You can see the nucleus anchored in the valley of the Nervión River, with all its width and neighborhoods. The height of the road gives a perfect panorama!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging 78-kilometer road cycling route is designed for experienced cyclists. It typically takes around 4 hours and 9 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as 'difficult' and requires a 'very good fitness' level due to its significant elevation gain of 1,360 meters over 78 kilometers. It features numerous climbs, including the Larreineta Climb, making it unsuitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, making it smooth for cycling. However, the terrain is characterized by rolling hills and substantial elevation changes, including specific ascents like the Larreineta Climb and Carretera a Bezi. You'll encounter a mix of cycleways, streets, and state roads.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, from views of the Bilbao estuary and Bilbao itself, to picturesque countryside. You'll pass traditional Basque farmhouses, forests, and charming villages like Sodupe, Güeñes, and Mercadillo. A notable stop is the Las Encartaciones Museum, offering cultural insights.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While the route can be enjoyed in various seasons, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring cooler, wetter conditions, especially in the higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route primarily follows public roads and cycleways. While there aren't specific restrictions against dogs on these public thoroughfares, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on a challenging 78 km racebike route due to the speed, traffic, and physical demands on the animal.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or need to pay entrance fees for this route, especially within Montes de Hierro?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and cycleways, including sections that pass through the Montes de Hierro region, which does not require specific cycling permits or entry fees.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Mercadillo, Gueñes and Sodupe route?
faqs.answer: As the route is a circular one starting from Bilbao, you can typically find parking in or around Bilbao city center or in the larger towns along the route like Sodupe, Güeñes, or Mercadillo. Look for public parking areas or designated street parking. Specific parking details for the exact trailhead are not provided, but starting from Bilbao offers various options.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting point of the route using public transport?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Bilbao, which is well-connected by public transport. You can reach Bilbao via train, bus, or even by flying into Bilbao Airport. From within Bilbao, local buses or the metro can take you to various points close to the route's starting area, allowing you to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route coincide with other trails. Notably, it covers parts of the Camino Olvidado (Bilbao-Villasante), the Vía Verde del Kadagua, and the Northern Route (Basque Country) of Saint James, offering a blend of local and long-distance cycling experiences.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several charming villages like Sodupe, Güeñes, and Mercadillo, you will find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel. Accommodation options are also available in these towns and, of course, extensively in Bilbao.
